data.title: RED: Write failing test for timestamp ordering of LINK/UNLINK events in process_outbound
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Write a failing test verifying that LINK and UNLINK events within a sync batch are processed in timestamp order. Currently process_outbound processes events in the order they appear in the event list. For LINK/UNLINK, out-of-order processing can cause incorrect state (e.g., UNLINK before LINK creates a stale delete attempt).

## Test Approach
Given: A batch of events containing LINK and UNLINK events with timestamps [t3, t1, t2] (out of order), all with relation="relates_to"
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: The events are processed in timestamp order (t1, t2, t3), verified by checking the order of acli_client method calls

Given: A batch with mixed event types (CREATE at t2, LINK at t1, STATUS at t3)
When: process_outbound() is called
Then: LINK/UNLINK events are sorted by timestamp; non-LINK events maintain their original order

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py (Edit: add timestamp ordering tests)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test functions verify LINK/UNLINK timestamp ordering
  Verify: grep -q "timestamp.*order\|sort.*link" tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py
- [ ] Tests fail (RED — ordering not yet implemented)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "timestamp_order" --tb=short -q 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED\|ERROR"
